hints: A correlated subquery references a column from the outer query, so it re-runs for each outer row. Here the outer row is an employee (aliased e); you want the inner AVG to cover only that employee's branch.
Inside the subquery, add WHERE x.branch_id = e.branch_id. The e.branch_id reaches back to the current outer row, so each employee is measured against their own branch's average.

Full shape: SELECT first_name, salary, branch_id FROM employee e WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee x WHERE x.branch_id = e.branch_id) ORDER BY employee_id; Only Michael (90000 vs Scranton's 62400) clears his branch's bar.
